questions.questionText: Which of the following alcohols will not undergo oxidation?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution: Because it is a tertiary alcohol and tertiary alcohol do not under go oxidation, it undergo dehydration and make alkene.

questions.questionText: Which one of the following has lowest $$\mathrm{pK}_{\mathrm{a}}$$ value?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution: $$\mathrm{O}_2 \mathrm{N}-\mathrm{CH}_2-\mathrm{COOH}$$ having less value of $${\mathrm{p}} \mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{a}}$$ because its acidic strength is very high.

questions.questionText:
Assertion (A): Acetic acid but not formic acid can be halogenated in presence of red $$\mathrm{P}$$ and $$\mathrm{Cl}_2$$.

Reason (R): Acetic acid is a weaker acid than formic acid.
options:
Both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) are true and Reason (R) is the correct explanation of the Assertion (A).
Both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) and true, but Reason (R) is not the correct explanation of the Assertion (A).
Assertion (A) is true, but Reason (R) is false.
Assertion (A) is false, but Reason (R) is true.
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution: Because acetic acid have hydrogen atoms which can be replaced by halogen. But formic acid do not have.

questions.questionText:
For the pair phenol and cyclohexanol, answer the following

(a) Why is phenol more acidic than cyclohexanol?

(b) Give one chemical test to distinguish between the two.
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
(a) Cyclohexanol is less acidic as compared to phenol because phenol is an aromatic compound while cyclohexanol is cyclic ring structure containing hydroxyl ion (-OH group).

In phenol benzene ring is having double bond and shows $\mathrm{sp}^2$ hybridization. Because of more s-character it is more acidic.

(b) When phenol react with Ferric Chloride, it gives violet colour but cyclohexanol remains colourless when react with $$\mathrm{FeCl}_3$$.

questions.questionText:
(a) (i) Why is the $$\mathrm{C}-\mathrm{O}$$ bond length in phenols less than that in methanol?

(ii) Arrange the following in order of increasing boiling point:

Ethoxyethane, Butanal, Butanol, n-butane

(iii) How can phenol be prepared from anisole? Give reaction.

OR

(b) (i) Give mechanism of the following reaction:

$$\mathrm{CH}_3-\mathrm{CH}_2-\mathrm{OH} \xrightarrow[413 \mathrm{~K}]{\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4} \mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{CH}_2-\mathrm{O} -\mathrm{CH}_2 \mathrm{CH}_3+\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$$

(ii) Illustrate hydroboration - oxidation reaction with an example.
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
(a) (i) C - O bond length in phenol is less than methanol because of presence of benzene ring which is aromatic and consisting of double bond. The lone pair present in oxygen is shared with partial conjugation effect while in methanol the lone pair of oxygen shared with normal carbon atom.

(ii) Butanol > Butane > Butanal > Ethoxy ethane

(iii)

OR

(ii) Hydroboration -

questions.questionText:
(a) (i) Write the reaction involved in Cannizaro's reaction.

(ii) Why are the boiling point of aldehydes and ketones lower than that of corresponding carboxylic acids?

(iii) An organic compound '$$\mathrm{A}$$' with molecular formula $$\mathrm{C}_5 \mathrm{H}_8 \mathrm{O}_2$$ is reduced to $$\mathrm{n}$$-pentane with hydrazone followed by heating with $$\mathrm{NaOH}$$ and Glycol. 'A' forms a dioxime with hydroxylamine and gives a positive Iodoform and Tollen's test. Identify '$$A$$' and give its reaction for Iodoform and Tollen's test.

OR

(b) (i) Give a chemical test to distinguish between ethanol acid and ethanoic acid.

(ii) Why is the $$\alpha$$-hydrogens of aldehydes and ketones are acidic in nature?

(iii) An organic compound '$$\mathrm{A}$$' with molecular formula $$\mathrm{C}_4 \mathrm{H}_8 \mathrm{O}_2$$ undergoes acid hydrolysis to form two compounds '$$\mathrm{B}$$' and '$$\mathrm{C}$$'. Oxidation of '$$C$$' with acidified potassium permanganate also produces 'B'. Sodium salt of 'B' on heating with soda lime gives methane.

(1) Identify 'A', 'B' and 'C'.

(2) Out of 'B' and 'C', which will have higher boiling point? Give reason.
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
Cannizaro Reaction (a) (i)

(ii) Boiling points of aldehyde and ketones are less than carboxylic acid because carboxylic acid have hydrogen bonding and associated molecules bonding which increases the boiling point of carboxylic acids.

(iii)

(b) (i) Sodium bicarbonate $$\left(\mathrm{NaHCO}_3\right)$$ is used to distinguish ethanol and ethanoic acid ethanoic acid give brick Red fumes with sodium bicarbonate. ethanol do not react with sodium bicarbonate.

(ii) $$\alpha$$-Hydrogen is always attached with oxygen which is highly electronegative and having lone pair of electrons. Alpha hydrogen atom when donate electron it act as an acid.

(iii)

1. A - CH$$_3$$ - COOC$$_2$$H$$_5$$ (ethyl methanoate)

$$\mathrm{B}$$ - $$\mathrm{CH}_3-\mathrm{COOH}$$ (ethanoic acid)

$$\mathrm{C}$$ - $$\mathrm{CH}_3-\mathrm{COONa}$$ (sodium acetate)

2. Ethanoic acid will have higher boiling point because it contain associate molecule which participate in hydrogen bonding. H-bonding is strong bonding which required high temperature to boil.

questions.questionText: Which of the following would not be a good choice for reducing nitrobenzene to aniline?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution: Nitrobenzene gives azo product when react with $$\mathrm{LiAlH}_4$$. Nitrobenzene react with highly reactive metal and conc. $$\mathrm{HCl}$$ to give Aniline.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution: Hydrolysis of sucrose (sugarcane) is called inversion. On undergoing hydrolysis it gives equimolar amount of glucose and fructose.

questions.questionText:
Assertion (A): Proteins are polymers of $$\alpha$$-amino acids connected by a peptide bond.

Reason (R): A tetrapeptide contains 4 amino acids linked by 4 peptide bonds.
options:
Both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) are true and Reason (R) is the correct explanation of the Assertion (A).
Both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) and true, but Reason (R) is not the correct explanation of the Assertion (A).
Assertion (A) is true, but Reason (R) is false.
Assertion (A) is false, but Reason (R) is true.
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution: Assertion is true but reason is false because in tetrapeptide, four amino acid and three peptide bonds are present.

questions.questionText:
The following experimental rate data were obtained for a reaction carried out at $$25^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$$

$$A_{(g)}+B_{(g)} \rightarrow C_{(g)}+D_{(g)}$$

Initial $$\mathrm{[A_{(g)}]/mol~dm^{-3}}$$ Initial $$\mathrm{[B_{(g)}]/mol~dm^{-3}}$$ Initial rate/mol $$\mathrm{dm^{-3}s^{-1}}$$
$$3.0 \times 10^{-2}$$ $$2.0 \times 10^{-2}$$ $$1.89 \times 10^{-4}$$
$$3.0 \times 10^{-2}$$ $$4.0 \times 10^{-2}$$ $$1.89 \times 10^{-4}$$
$$6.0 \times 10^{-2}$$ $$4.0 \times 10^{-2}$$ $$7.56 \times 10^{-4}$$

$$\text { What are the orders with respect to } \mathrm{A}_{(\mathrm{g})} \text { and } \mathrm{B}_{(\mathrm{g})} \text { ? }$$
options:
Order with respect to $$\mathrm{A_{(g)}}$$ Order with respect to $$\mathrm{B_{(g)}}$$
Zero Second

Order with respect to $$\mathrm{A_{(g)}}$$ Order with respect to $$\mathrm{B_{(g)}}$$
First Zero

Order with respect to $$\mathrm{A_{(g)}}$$ Order with respect to $$\mathrm{B_{(g)}}$$
Second Zero

Order with respect to $$\mathrm{A_{(g)}}$$ Order with respect to $$\mathrm{B_{(g)}}$$
Second First
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
To determine the order of the reaction with respect to each reactant ($$\mathrm{A}_{(\mathrm{g})}$$ and $$\mathrm{B}_{(\mathrm{g})}$$), we'll analyze the rate data from the table:

Initial Rates and Concentrations :

1. $$[A_{(g)}] = 3.0 \times 10^{-2} \text{ mol dm}^{-3}, [B_{(g)}] = 2.0 \times 10^{-2} \text{ mol dm}^{-3}, \text{rate} = 1.89 \times 10^{-4} \text{ mol dm}^{-3}\text{s}^{-1}$$

2. $$[A_{(g)}] = 3.0 \times 10^{-2} \text{ mol dm}^{-3}, [B_{(g)}] = 4.0 \times 10^{-2} \text{ mol dm}^{-3}, \text{rate} = 1.89 \times 10^{-4} \text{ mol dm}^{-3}\text{s}^{-1}$$

3. $$[A_{(g)}] = 6.0 \times 10^{-2} \text{ mol dm}^{-3}, [B_{(g)}] = 4.0 \times 10^{-2} \text{ mol dm}^{-3}, \text{rate} = 7.56 \times 10^{-4} \text{ mol dm}^{-3}\text{s}^{-1}$$

Comparing Experiments 1 and 2 (to determine the order with respect to $$\mathrm{B}_{(\mathrm{g})}$$):

- The concentration of $$\mathrm{A}_{(\mathrm{g})}$$ is constant.

- The concentration of $$\mathrm{B}_{(\mathrm{g})}$$ is doubled.

- The rate remains the same.

This suggests that changing the concentration of $$\mathrm{B}_{(\mathrm{g})}$$ has no effect on the rate, meaning the order with respect to $$\mathrm{B}_{(\mathrm{g})}$$ is zero.

Comparing Experiments 2 and 3 (to determine the order with respect to $$\mathrm{A}_{(\mathrm{g})}$$):

- The concentration of $$\mathrm{A}_{(\mathrm{g})}$$ is doubled ($$3.0 \times 10^{-2}$$ to $$6.0 \times 10^{-2}$$).

- The concentration of $$\mathrm{B}_{(\mathrm{g})}$$ remains constant.

- The rate increases by a factor of 4 ($$1.89 \times 10^{-4}$$ to $$7.56 \times 10^{-4}$$).

Because doubling $$\mathrm{A}_{(\mathrm{g})}$$ results in the rate being quadrupled, it suggests that the order with respect to $$\mathrm{A}_{(\mathrm{g})}$$ is two (second order).

The correct orders are:

Order with respect to $$\mathrm{A_{(g)}}$$: Second

Order with respect to $$\mathrm{B_{(g)}}$$: Zero

Hence, the correct option is C :

Order with respect to $$\mathrm{A_{(g)}}$$ Order with respect to $$\mathrm{B_{(g)}}$$
Second Zero
